channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Ben Roberts-Smith Arrested for Alleged War Crimes in Afghanistan
item.description: Ben Roberts-Smith, Australia's most decorated living soldier, was arrested at Sydney Airport in relation to alleged war crimes committed during his service in Afghanistan. The Australian Federal Polic...

item.title: NASA Artemis II Mission Makes Historic Flyby of the Moon’s Far Side
item.description: NASA’s Artemis II mission has marked a historic moment in space exploration as astronauts became the furthest humans from Earth, passing the Moon’s far side. The four-person crew, including Reid Wisem...

item.title: Zanzibar Marks 54 Years Since Karume's Assassination with National Prayers
item.description: Sasa, rais Samia Suluhu Hassan na rais Hussein Mwinyi wanatarajia kumuongoza wananchi na viongozi wakuu katika sala za taifa Kisiwandui, kujumlisha kumbukumbu ya miaka 54 tangu kuuawa kwake Rais Abeid...

item.title: Ngong-Suswa Highway Opens, Boosting Travel and Trade in Western Kenya
item.description: The recently completed Ngong-Suswa Highway is set to transform regional transport and economic activity in Kenya. Interior Principal Secretary Raymond Omollo has described the approximately 70-kilomet...

item.title: Female Police Officer Files Administrative Case Against Provincial Director for Alleged Misconduct
item.description: A female police officer has formally filed administrative complaints against a high-ranking official in the Philippine National Police. The case targets the police provincial director over claims of a...

item.title: More than 10 people die on Eastern Cape roads over Easter weekend
item.description: The Eastern Cape’s roads have claimed more than 10 lives since the start of the Easter holiday period, according to the provincial Department of Transport. The deadliest of the accidents occurred on G...

item.title: Minister Katambi urges calm and warns against false genital theft claims after mob killing in Rukwa
item.description: Minister for Home Affairs, Mr Patrobas Katambi, has issued a firm warning to Tanzanians against circulating rumours that people’s private parts are being stolen. Speaking in Dar es Salaam, Mr Katambi ...

item.title: Baby Born on Flight from Jamaica to New York Raises Citizenship Questions
item.description: A Caribbean Airlines passenger flying from Kingston, Jamaica, to New York City gave birth while the plane was on its final approach to John F. Kennedy International Airport. Both the mother and her ne...

item.title: Israeli airstrike in Gaza kills four amid ceasefire tensions
item.description: On Sunday, an Israeli airstrike in the northern Gaza Strip killed four Palestinians and injured others, according to local health authorities. The strike targeted a group of people in Jaffa Street, ne...

item.title: Indian Startup Rocket Uses AI to Deliver Affordable McKinsey-Style Product Strategies
item.description: Rocket, an AI startup based in Surat, India, has launched a new platform aimed at revolutionizing product strategy and consulting. The platform, Rocket 1.0, combines research, product development, and...

item.title: Kenyan Skater Kevin Kiarie Wins Big in Benin Despite Lack of Government Support
item.description: Kevin Kiarie Rohio, a young Kenyan skater, made history at the April 2026 International Skating Challenge in Cotonou, Benin. He won both gold and silver in the competition, a remarkable achievement co...

item.title: Lady Gaga forced to cancel Montreal concert due to worsening respiratory infection
item.description: Lady Gaga was forced to cancel her Montreal concert on 6th April 2026, just hours before she was due to take the stage, citing a worsening respiratory infection. The 40-year-old Grammy-winning singer ...

item.title: Trump Mobilizes Cabinet Members to Support Republican Midterm Campaigns While Minimizing Controversial Issues
item.description: President Donald Trump is deploying top Cabinet members and aides to actively participate in Republican midterm campaigns, particularly in competitive districts. The goal is to emphasize popular achie...

item.title: Family of Indian Seafarer Killed in Iranian Attack Seeks DNA Test of Remains Amid Confusion
item.description: The family of Dixit Solanki, an Indian seafarer killed in an Iranian drone strike off the Oman coast, has approached the Bombay High Court demanding a DNA test of his remains. Solanki, an engine room ...

item.title: Artemis II Mission Surpasses Apollo 13 Distance Record
item.description: The Artemis II mission has achieved a historic milestone by sending humans farther from Earth than ever before, surpassing the previous record set by the Apollo 13 crew, which reached 248,655 miles fr...

item.title: Pakistan Sees Growing Shift to Electric Motorbikes Amid Petrol Crisis
item.description: Due to the recent crisis in the Middle East, especially the Iran blockage of the Strait of Hormuz after attacks by US and Israel, Pakistanis are facing soaring fuel prices and fears of petrol shortage...

item.title: Embracing Inclusivity: How Gender Equality Strengthened My Business and Life
item.description: Guy Singh-Watson, founder of Riverford, reflects on how traditional masculinity limited his personal and professional growth. On International Women’s Day, he attended an event to listen to women’s ex...

item.title: Practical Ways for Freelancers to Negotiate Fairly and Professionally
item.description: This article by Charlotte Makala offers practical guidance for freelancers on how to negotiate in a way that maintains good client relationships. It begins by explaining a common mistake many freelanc...

item.title: BJP and Opposition Battle Over Welfare Schemes and Local Issues in Upper Assam
item.description: In Upper Assam, the upcoming Assembly elections have highlighted a significant contest between the ruling BJP and a Congress-led Opposition alliance. Voters are heavily influenced by the BJP’s extensi...

item.title: Tanzania strengthens role in Africa’s cross-border payment systems
item.description: Tanzania is making important steps in boosting its place in Africa’s financial systems by linking with regional payment platforms. The Bank of Tanzania (BoT) Governor, Mr Emmanuel Tutuba, said this de...
